message:
fix(run_all_tests): handle empty test array in discover_tests

Add guard to prevent printing empty array which outputs just a newline.
The `printf '%s\n' "${tests[@]}"` with an empty array would output a
blank line, which could confuse consumers parsing the output.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -153,7 +153,8 @@ discover_tests() {
         done < <(find "$SCRIPT_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name 'test_*.sh' -type f -print0 | sort -z)
     fi
 
-    printf '%s\n' "${tests[@]}"
+    # Only print if there are tests (empty array would output just newline)
+    [[ ${#tests[@]} -gt 0 ]] && printf '%s\n' "${tests[@]}"
 }
 
 #==============================================================================
